page: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core, and is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is delivered via a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results show a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, scoring 72830.09, which places it in the top 25% tier. However, single-core performance is relatively modest, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores falling into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached small-block reads reaching 2838.7 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 167.78 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 10% tier at 4.56 tokens/sec, though medium-model (7B) text generation remains average at 19.71 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6g.12xlarge leverages the ARM64 architecture to offer high physical core density and strong multi-threaded throughput without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. This makes it appropriate for highly parallelized workloads that scale across its 48 dedicated cores. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks. It is less effective for single-threaded applications or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on AWS Graviton processors running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 96.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU capabilities, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng multi-core tests, though single-core performance and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression fall into the bottom 25%. The instance offers a cost-efficient profile for parallelized workloads due to its dedicated physical core density. It is optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton processors, 96 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton processors, 96 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core ARM64 compute instance delivering strong multi-threaded performance and balanced memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz on AWS Graviton processors.
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks.
Slower single-core and single-threaded compression performance.
Appropriate for software compilation, static web serving, and parallel database tasks.
